MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Margaret Blodgett, Esq. 925-258-0500 of Mastrangelo Law Offices for Plaintiff.
Josh Friedman, Esq. of Collins Collins Muir & Stewart for Defendant Burlingame Point et al.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF GARY STAPLETON's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses to Plaintiff's Request for Production Nos. 1-4 from Defendant Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Company (CCP 2031.310)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The judge pro tem's well-reasoned recommendation is adopted; plaintiff Gary Stapleton's motion to compel defendants' accident scene photographs is granted. Such photos are not communications, and thus not attorney-client communications. (Suezaki v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 58 Cal.2d 166, 176.) The photographs are arguably attorney work product, but are discoverable under CCP 2018.030(b). Plaintiff could not take his own accident scene photographs, for he was severely injured, and the trench he fell into was filled soon after defendants' photos were taken. The parties have resolved their other discovery disputes.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Josh Friedman, Esq. of Collins Collins Muir + Stewart LLP, T:626.243.1100, for defendant and moving party;
Edward Mastrangelo, Esq. and Margaret Blodgette, Esq. of Mastrangelo Law Offices, T:925.258.0500, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT AZUL WORKS HOLDING INC.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint as follows:

Defendant Azul Works Holding Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff Gary Stapleton's first amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part.

The allegations related to Mr. Debarro's apology, as pleading evidentiary facts, and the improper citation to a superseded Court of Appeal opinion, are stricken from the pleadings as improper matter, as plaintiff concedes.

The motion is also granted with respect to the allegations of cross-agency and employment, because these boilerplate allegations are factually inconsistent with other allegations in the complaint. The ruling is without prejudice to plaintiff seeking leave to amend to add allegations as to the relationship among the various defendants after conducting discovery.

The motion is denied as to the request to strike the negligence per se cause of action, and is also denied as to the conclusory allegations of negligence, which are sufficiently supported by factual allegations. (See Perkins v. Superior Court (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 6 ["the courts have permitted allegations which obviously included conclusions of law and have termed them 'ultimate facts' or 'conclusions of facts.' " [Citations]. What is important is that the complaint as a whole contain sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief."].)

Counsel for the plaintiff shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEFENDANT HATHAWAY DINWIDDIE CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Venue.

Defendants Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Company and Critchfield Mechanical, Inc.'s motion to change venue based on the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 397 is denied without prejudice as premature. Such a motion may not be brought until all defendants have answered. (Code Civ. Proc. 396b(a); Cholakian & Associates v.
